2605.21154.title: Automated ICD Classification of Psychiatric Diagnoses: From Classical NLP to Large Language Models
2605.21154.paper_summary: Mental health has become a global priority, leading to a massive administrative burden in the coding of clinical diagnoses. This study proposes the automation of psychiatric diagnostic analysis by mapping free-text descriptions to the International Classification of Diseases (ICD) using Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Machine Learning (ML) techniques. Utilizing a specialized dataset of 145,513 Spanish psychiatric descriptions, various text representation paradigms were evaluated, ranging from classical frequency-based models (BoW, TF-IDF) to state-of-the-art Large Language Models (LLMs) such as e5\_large, BioLORD, and Llama-3-8B. Results indicate that transformer-based embeddings consistently outperform traditional methods by capturing implicit semantic cues and nuanced medical terminology. The e5\_large model, through end-to-end fine-tuning, achieved the highest performance with a $F1_{micro}$ score of 0.866. This research demonstrates that adapting LLMs to specific clinical nomenclature is essential for overcoming the challenges of ``long-tail'' label distributions and the inherent ambiguity of psychiatric discourse.
2605.21154.paper_summary_zh: 心理健康已成為全球優先事項，導致臨床診斷編碼的巨大行政負擔。這項研究提出通過使用自然語言處理（NLP）和機器學習（ML）技術將自由文本描述映射到國際疾病分類（ICD）來自動化精神病診斷分析。利用一個包含145,513個西班牙精神病描述的專門數據集，評估了各種文本表示範式，從傳統的基於頻率的模型（BoW、TF-IDF）到最先進的大型語言模型（LLMs），如e5\_large、BioLORD和Llama-3-8B。結果表明，基於Transformer的嵌入在捕捉隱含語義線索和細微醫學術語方面，始終優於傳統方法。通過端到端的微調，e5\_large模型達到了最高性能，$F1_{micro}$得分為0.866。這項研究表明，將LLMs適應特定的臨床命名法對於克服“長尾”標籤分佈的挑戰和精神病話語固有的模糊性至關重要。
2605.21154.author: Fernando Ortega et.al.
2605.21154.authors: Fernando Ortega, Raúl Lara-Cabrera, Jorge Dueñas-Lerín, Alejandro de la Torre-Luque, Mercé Salvador Robert, Enrique Baca-García
